title: Cheap Houses for Rent in West Park, Cleveland
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in West Park, Cleveland
metaDescription: Looking for cheap houses for rent in West Park, Cleveland? See typical rent ranges, transit options, and how to tell our team what you need.
primaryKeyword: cheap houses for rent West Park cleveland
secondaryKeywords: cheap rentals far west side cleveland
answerBox: West Park is the collective name for Cleveland's far west-side neighborhoods — Kamm's Corners, Jefferson, and Bellaire-Puritas — near the Cleveland Hopkins Airport corridor. Citywide, Cleveland's median asking rent was $1,250 a month as of Zumper's July 2026 report, with lower-cost houses commonly found below that figure.

sections.h2: Renting a cheap house in West Park
body: West Park is not a single official neighborhood but a well-known umbrella term for three of Cleveland's far west-side Statistical Planning Areas: **Kamm's Corners**, **Jefferson**, and **Bellaire-Puritas**. Together they sit along the far western edge of the city, near the Cleveland Hopkins International Airport corridor and Interstate 71.

Kamm's Corners is the area's main commercial node, centered on Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive, with a mix of small shops and older residential streets branching off in every direction. Jefferson and Bellaire-Puritas are largely residential, with housing stock dating mostly from the early-to-mid 20th century — modest single-family homes, bungalows, and a smaller share of duplexes compared to Cleveland's east side.

We don't currently have a large concentration of listings specifically in West Park, but we manage 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes across the greater Cleveland area and add inventory regularly. If a cheap house in this part of the city is what you're after, the fastest path is to [tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) so we can match you with something that fits, here or nearby.

sections.h2: How much does a cheap house cost in Cleveland?
body: "Cheap" is relative, so it helps to see the citywide range. Per Zumper's rent report dated July 4, 2026, Cleveland's overall median asking rent is **$1,250 a month**, up 4.2% year-over-year — and Zumper puts Cleveland roughly 36% below the national median of $1,950. A separate report from RentCafe (Yardi), dated July 2, 2026, tracks a higher **average of $1,564** for professionally managed apartment buildings specifically, which tend to skew larger and newer than the individual houses and duplexes common on the far west side.

For comparison, the rental homes we manage across Greater Cleveland — mostly 2- and 3-bedroom houses and duplexes — typically run from about **$700 to $1,800 a month**, with a company-wide typical rent around **$1,000**. Every home in that portfolio accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready.

West Park is served by Greater Cleveland RTA bus routes connecting Kamm's Corners and the surrounding streets to Downtown, and its location near Cleveland Hopkins International Airport puts it close to the RTA **Red Line**, the only U.S. rapid-transit line that runs directly from a downtown core to its city's main airport. Lorain Avenue functions as the area's primary east-west commercial corridor.

items: **Area:** Kamm's Corners, Jefferson, and Bellaire-Puritas Statistical Planning Areas
**Transit:** RTA bus routes to Downtown; near the Red Line and Cleveland Hopkins Airport
**Commercial hub:** Lorain Avenue at Rocky River Drive (Kamm's Corners)

sections.h2: Is Section 8 accepted for houses in West Park?
body: Acceptance of Housing Choice Vouchers in Cleveland depends on the individual landlord. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and as of this writing, the City of Cleveland itself has not enacted an ordinance protecting voucher holders from being turned away for using Section 8 — a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs (including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights) have their own local protections, but the city proper does not. Renters should confirm a landlord's voucher policy directly before applying.

Every home we manage across the Cleveland area accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of neighborhood. For background on how the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) voucher program works, see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) or the [CMHA housing choice voucher page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php).

sections.h2: Finding an affordable house near West Park
body: Because our current listings are concentrated elsewhere in Cleveland, the quickest way to find a cheap house near West Park (or a comparably priced home in a nearby area we do serve) is to reach out directly with your budget, bedroom needs, and timeline. You can also browse our broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) page or the [West Park neighborhood overview](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-west-park-cleveland/) for more context on the area.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have houses for rent in West Park?
faq.a: Our current inventory is concentrated in other parts of Cleveland, so West Park listings are limited at any given time. We manage 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes across Greater Cleveland and add inventory regularly, so it's worth telling our team your budget and bedroom needs — we may have something nearby that fits.

faq.q: What's the cheapest a house rents for in Cleveland?
faq.a: Per Zumper's July 2026 report, Cleveland's citywide median rent is $1,250 a month, with studios starting around $1,060. Our own portfolio of managed rental homes across Greater Cleveland ranges from about $700 to $1,800 a month, with a typical rent around $1,000.

faq.q: What neighborhoods make up West Park?
faq.a: West Park is a common name for three of Cleveland's official Statistical Planning Areas on the far west side: Kamm's Corners, Jefferson, and Bellaire-Puritas. It sits near the Cleveland Hopkins International Airport corridor and Interstate 71.

faq.q: Is Section 8 accepted in West Park, Cleveland?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord. Cleveland has not enacted a citywide source-of-income protection ordinance, so landlords can legally decline vouchers, though some Cuyahoga County suburbs do protect voucher holders. Every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers regardless of neighborhood.

faq.q: How do I get around West Park without a car?
faq.a: RTA bus routes connect Kamm's Corners and the surrounding streets to Downtown, and the area is close to the Red Line, which links directly to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport — the only U.S. rapid-transit line to do so.
